message:
feat(notifications): Cx-first handle_detection_with_cx entry point (ft-xbnl0.2.3)

Adds NotificationPipeline::handle_detection_with_cx behind the
asupersync-runtime feature, extending the Cx-first migration pattern
already landed across EventWaiter, NativeEventListener, SnapshotEngine
(capture_with_cx, ensure_session_with_cx, cleanup_with_cx,
shutdown_checkpoint_with_cx), PaneWaiter, WorkflowRunner,
SearchBridge, CautClient, SurvivalModel, MuxWatchdog, RestoreProcess,
TelemetryCollector, Tailer, IPC primitives, RecordingManager,
memory_pressure, memory_budget, cpu_pressure, orphan_reaper,
protocol_recovery, pool acquire, metrics, cass search, wezterm
list_panes / get_pane / send_text / get_pane_direction /
pane_tiered_scrollback_summary / get_text / wait_for /
wait_for_codex_session_summary, broadcast/oneshot senders, and the
Mutex/RwLock primitives.

Semantics:

  * Internal mute_store RwLock acquire uses read_with_cx(cx) from
    runtime_compat (landed in tick 9), so a caller-cancelled mute
    check propagates cleanly through the lock wait instead of
    blocking on the ambient lock guard.
  * storage_guard.is_event_muted and sender.send() still flow
    through ambient Cx because StorageHandle and the
    NotificationSender trait are not yet Cx-first at the trait
    level. These are scheduled for later ticks.
  * Return type and downstream decision/dispatch behavior mirror
    handle_detection exactly — NotifyDecision::Filtered for muted
    events, NotifyDecision::Send {...} for deliverable events,
    dispatch_payload for rendering + fanout.

Test: pipeline_with_cx_mute_store_blocks_muted_events — pins that a
fresh, non-cancelled Cx with a populated mute_store produces the
same NotifyDecision::Filtered outcome as the legacy entry point, so
the Cx-first wrapper does not regress mute-gate behavior on the
happy path.

The legacy handle_detection entry point is preserved for
non-migrated callers; this change is strictly additive.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
